name: Thyme-Fig Fruitcake
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the apple juice, thyme, and figs in a bowl. Set aside for 10 minutes.
2. Stir together the flour, cornmeal, baking powder, and baking soda in a separate bowl.
3. Beat the oil, sugar, and egg until well blended. Pour this mixture into the dry ingredients.
4. Add the pine nuts and the thyme-fig mixture to the batter. Mix thoroughly.
5. Pour the batter into an oiled and floured 9-inch round (or 8 inch square) baking pan.
6. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 40 to 50 minutes.
7. Allow the cake to cool in the pan for 5 minutes before removing it. Then, cool it thoroughly on a wire rack.
Recipe details:
- 1/12 recipe - 190 calories, 1 bread, 1 fruit, 1 1/2 fat exchange
- 27 grams carbohydrate, 3 grams protein, 8 grams fat
- 66 mg sodium, 156 mg potassium, 23 mg cholesterol
